# Epic 3 Retrospective: Social & Friends

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Epic:** Epic 3 - Social & Friends
**Status:** Complete (3/3 stories done - 100%)
**Facilitator:** Bob (Scrum Master) | **Mode:** YOLO Auto-Generated

---

## Epic Summary

Epic 3 implemented the social layer: user search, friend requests (send/accept/decline/cancel), friends list with real-time online status using Supabase Presence.

**Stories Completed:**

1. 3-1 User Search & Send Friend Request
2. 3-2 Friend Request Management
3. 3-3 Friends List with Online Status

**Test Growth:** 180 -> 259 tests (79 new tests)

---

## Successes & Strengths

1. **Canonical ordering pattern for friendships:** `user_a < user_b` canonical ordering prevented duplicate friendship rows -- a clean, robust data integrity pattern.
2. **First real-time feature (Presence):** Story 3-3 successfully introduced Supabase Presence for online status, establishing the real-time architecture that Epic 5 built on extensively.
3. **Core/wrapper separation pattern:** Splitting `presence-core.ts` (testable logic) from `presence.svelte.ts` (reactive wrapper) enabled excellent testability. This pattern became canonical for all subsequent real-time features.
4. **Reusable components with forward-looking design:** `PlayerCard` and `OnlineIndicator` components created in 3-1 were reused across subsequent epics.
5. **Cross-model review (GPT-5 reviewing Claude's code):** Story 3-3 used a different AI model for review, catching accessibility and hardening issues that same-model review might miss.

---

## Challenges & Growth Areas

1. **CSS class usage without definition:** Story 3-2 had `.empty-message` class used in template but not defined in scoped styles. Easy mistake with Svelte scoped CSS.
2. **Broken aria-labelledby references:** Heading conditionally rendered inside `{#if}` but referenced by `aria-labelledby` from outside -- screen readers broke when list was empty.
3. **Color-only status communication:** Online status initially communicated only by dot color (green/gray), failing accessibility. Fixed by adding screen reader text.
4. **Stale callback cleanup:** Presence channel callbacks needed explicit cleanup on lobby leave to prevent memory leaks.

---

## Key Insights & Learnings

- Canonical ordering (`user_a < user_b`) for symmetric relationships prevents duplicate rows -- critical data integrity pattern.
- Separating "core logic" from "reactive wrapper" enables testability for Svelte 5 runes-based code.
- Defensive copies of mutable state (Sets) prevent bugs from external mutation.
- `aria-labelledby` references break when the target element is conditionally rendered.
- Online status communicated only by color is not accessible -- screen readers need text alternatives.
- Presence channel reconnection needs to handle `track()` failures, not just subscription failures.
- Debouncing (300ms) prevents excessive search queries.

---

## Technical Debt Incurred

| Item                                                       | Severity | Status   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | -------- |
| Pre-existing env var type errors (PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/KEY) | Low      | Persists |
| Unused i18n keys accumulation                              | Low      | Ongoing  |

---

## Action Items

1. **Verify CSS class definitions against template usage** -- add to code review checklist.
2. **Keep aria-referenced elements always in DOM** -- never put aria targets inside conditional blocks.
3. **Cross-model code review** proved valuable -- consider using different AI models for review when available.

---

## Breakthrough

The core/wrapper separation pattern (`-core.ts` + `.svelte.ts`) established here became the definitive architecture for all real-time features in Epic 5. This single decision dramatically improved testability across the project.
